mcq.question_md: You add a dependency and build. Maven downloads the jar once — and your next build, even in a *different* project needing the same jar, downloads nothing. Where did Maven put it?
options: In the local repository (the .m2 folder in your home directory), shared by all your projects
Inside src/main/resources of the project that asked for it
Nowhere — Maven streams dependencies from Maven Central on every build
mcq.explanation_md: Maven caches every downloaded artifact in your **local repository** — `~/.m2/repository` — organised by its coordinates. Every project on your machine shares that one cache, which is why the second project's build was instant. The tempting wrong answer is `target/`: that folder holds *your* build output (compiled classes, your jar) and is wiped by `mvn clean` — if dependencies lived there, every clean would mean re-downloading the internet. Streaming on every build would make offline work impossible; the cache is exactly what lets Maven work on hostel wifi.
